classes.name: cmlibs.widgets.groupeditorwidget.GroupEditorWidget
documentation:
This widget takes a list of Zinc groups as an input and provides a GUI allowing the user to redefine a specific group based on the
nodes/elements contained in each of the other groups in the list. Calls to the GroupEditorWidget constructor must ensure that the
group arguments are valid and non-empty.

This widget emits a group_updated signal when the group operations are applied.

```
param current_group

The FieldGroup to add elements to.

param group_list

A list of FieldGroups to use for redefining the current group.
```

modules.name: cmlibs.widgets.handlers.orientation
documentation:
Created: December, 2023

@author: tsalemink

methods.name: cmlibs.widgets.handlers.orientation.Orientation.__init__
documentation:
Orientation handler constructor.

:param Qt.Key key_code: Qt key code specifying a keyboard key.

methods.name: cmlibs.widgets.handlers.orientation.Orientation.set_model
documentation:
Set the model required by the handler. The model is in charge of tracking the rotation-point and surface-normal attributes and must
provide the following methods required by the handler: `get_plane`, `get_plane_region`. The `get_plane` method
should return a Zinc `Plane`. The `get_plane_region` method should return the Zinc region associated with the
surface graphic. `plane_nodes_coordinates` should return a list of lists, specifying the coordinates of the four corners of the
plane segment (currently only square plane segments are supported).

```
param model

Model providing Zinc surface definition.
```

methods.name: cmlibs.widgets.sceneviewerwidget.SceneviewerWidget.project
documentation:
Project the given point in global coordinates into window pixel coordinates
with the origin at the window's top left pixel.
Note the z pixel coordinate is a depth which is mapped so that -1 is
on the far clipping plane, and +1 is on the near clipping plane.

methods.name: cmlibs.widgets.sceneviewerwidget.SceneviewerWidget.unproject
documentation:
Unproject the given point in window pixel coordinates where the origin is
at the window's top left pixel into global coordinates.
Note the z pixel coordinate is a depth which is mapped so that -1 is
on the far clipping plane, and +1 is on the near clipping plane.

directly from the event in the parent widget.

documentation:
Helper method to set the current scene viewer to view everything

methods.name: cmlibs.widgets.sceneviewerwidget.SceneviewerWidget.paintGL
documentation:
Render the scene for this scene viewer.  The QOpenGLWidget has already set up the
correct OpenGL buffer for us so all we need do is render into it.  The scene viewer
will clear the background so any OpenGL drawing of your own needs to go after this
API call.
paintGL start

methods.name: cmlibs.widgets.sceneviewerwidget.SceneviewerWidget._zincSceneviewerEvent
documentation:
Process a scene viewer event.  The updateGL() method is called for a
repaint required event all other events are ignored.

modules.name: cmlibs.widgets.basesceneviewerwidget
documentation:
Zinc Sceneviewer Widget

Implements a Zinc Sceneviewer Widget on Python using PySide or PyQt,
which renders the Zinc Scene with OpenGL and allows interactive
transformation of the view.
Widget is derived from QtOpenGL.QGLWidget.

This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public
License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this
file, You can obtain one at http://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/.

methods.name: cmlibs.widgets.basesceneviewerwidget.BaseSceneviewerWidget.__init__
documentation:
Call the super class init functions, set the  Zinc context and the scene viewer handle to None.
Initialise other attributes that deal with selection and the rotation of the plane.

methods.name: cmlibs.widgets.basesceneviewerwidget.BaseSceneviewerWidget.set_context
documentation:
Sets the context for this Zinc scene viewer widget. Prompts creation of a new Zinc
scene viewer, once graphics are initialised.

methods.name: cmlibs.widgets.basesceneviewerwidget.BaseSceneviewerWidget.get_zinc_sceneviewer
documentation: Get the scene viewer for this ZincWidget.

methods.name: cmlibs.widgets.basesceneviewerwidget.BaseSceneviewerWidget.set_selection_filter
documentation: Set filter to be applied in logical AND with sceneviewer filter during selection

methods.name: cmlibs.widgets.basesceneviewerwidget.BaseSceneviewerWidget.project
documentation:
Project the given point in global coordinates into window pixel coordinates
with the origin at the window's top left pixel.
Note the z pixel coordinate is a depth which is mapped so that -1 is
on the far clipping plane, and +1 is on the near clipping plane.

methods.name: cmlibs.widgets.basesceneviewerwidget.BaseSceneviewerWidget.unproject
documentation:
Unproject the given point in window pixel coordinates where the origin is
at the window's top left pixel into global coordinates.
Note the z pixel coordinate is a depth which is mapped so that -1 is
on the far clipping plane, and +1 is on the near clipping plane.

methods.name: cmlibs.widgets.basesceneviewerwidget.BaseSceneviewerWidget.view_all
documentation:
Helper method to set the current scene viewer to view everything
visible in the current scene.

methods.name: cmlibs.widgets.basesceneviewerwidget.BaseSceneviewerWidget._zinc_sceneviewer_event
documentation:
Process a scene viewer event.  The update() method is called for a
repaint required event all other events are ignored.

The OpenGL context is ready for use. If Zinc Context has been set, create Zinc Sceneviewer, otherwise
inform client who is required to set Context at a later time.

methods.name: cmlibs.widgets.basesceneviewerwidget.BaseSceneviewerWidget.paintGL
documentation:
Render the scene for this scene viewer.  The QOpenGLWidget has already set up the
correct OpenGL buffer for us so all we need do is render into it.  The scene viewer
will clear the background so any OpenGL drawing of your own needs to go after this
API call.
